item.title:
HORMUZ CLOSURE SENDS TANKER RATES SOARING
Tanker rates for very large crude carriers from the Middle East to Asia have hit record highs following the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, the EIA reports.
item.description:
HORMUZ CLOSURE SENDS TANKER RATES SOARING

Tanker rates for very large crude carriers from the Middle East to Asia have hit record highs following the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, the EIA reports.

item.title:
🚨 WHITE HOUSE CAUTIOUSLY HOPEFUL ON IRAN TALKS
White House officials are cautiously optimistic that recent negotiations with Iran are progressing, CBS reports. Pakistan is acting as an intermediary with Iran’s top security officials, not just the foreign ministry, increasing the chances of meaningful dialogue, sources say.
item.description:
🚨 WHITE HOUSE CAUTIOUSLY HOPEFUL ON IRAN TALKS

White House officials are cautiously optimistic that recent negotiations with Iran are progressing, CBS reports. Pakistan is acting as an intermediary with Iran’s top security officials, not just the foreign ministry, increasing the chances of meaningful dialogue, sources say.

item.title: 🚨 WHITE HOUSE OFFICIALS CAUTIOUSLY HOPEFUL LATEST TALKS WITH IRAN ARE MAKING PROGRESS, SOURCES FAMILIAR WITH THE TALKS - CBS REPORTER
item.description: 🚨 WHITE HOUSE OFFICIALS CAUTIOUSLY HOPEFUL LATEST TALKS WITH IRAN ARE MAKING PROGRESS, SOURCES FAMILIAR WITH THE TALKS - CBS REPORTER

item.title:
WARSH FED CONFIRMATION STALLED AMID IRAN WAR AND POWELL DISPUTE
Kevin Warsh’s nomination to replace Jerome Powell as Fed chair remains “at an impasse,” sources say. Sen. Thom Tillis won’t advance Warsh until the DOJ resolves its investigation into Powell’s $2.5B Fed renovation. Powell may stay on temporarily, potentially blocking Trump’s plans for lower rates. Wall Street fears heightened market volatility amid the Iran war if Fed leadership is uncertain. The White House pushes for swift confirmation, citing Warsh’s credentials, but Tillis remains firm, leaving the central bank’s future—and its response to rising inflation and energy shocks—in limbo.
item.description:
WARSH FED CONFIRMATION STALLED AMID IRAN WAR AND POWELL DISPUTE

Kevin Warsh’s nomination to replace Jerome Powell as Fed chair remains “at an impasse,” sources say. Sen. Thom Tillis won’t advance Warsh until the DOJ resolves its investigation into Powell’s $2.5B Fed renovation. Powell may stay on temporarily, potentially blocking Trump’s plans for lower rates. Wall Street fears heightened market volatility amid the Iran war if Fed leadership is uncertain. The White House pushes for swift confirmation, citing Warsh’s credentials, but Tillis remains firm, leaving the central bank’s future—and its response to rising inflation and energy shocks—in limbo.

item.title: *US WARNS IRAN WAR MAY DELAY UKRAINE WEAPONS SHIPMENTS: POLITICO
item.description: *US WARNS IRAN WAR MAY DELAY UKRAINE WEAPONS SHIPMENTS: POLITICO

item.title:
🚨 IRAN'S RESPONSE TO U.S. PEACE PROPOSAL EXPECTED FRIDAY, SOURCES SAY
Iran is expected to reply Friday to the U.S. 15-point peace framework via intermediaries, sources told CBS News. The plan, delivered through Pakistan, aims to curb hostilities and secure negotiations. President Trump’s team—including Steve Witkoff, Marco Rubio, JD Vance, and Jared Kushner—is coordinating discussions. U.S. officials report progress, citing increased energy flow through the Strait of Hormuz. White House emphasizes diplomacy is ongoing while military operations continue. Iran’s UN mission declined comment. Regional actors, including Turkey, and the IAEA are also engaged, though U.S.-Iran talks remain indirect due to the lack of official diplomatic relations.
item.description:
🚨 IRAN'S RESPONSE TO U.S. PEACE PROPOSAL EXPECTED FRIDAY, SOURCES SAY

Iran is expected to reply Friday to the U.S. 15-point peace framework via intermediaries, sources told CBS News. The plan, delivered through Pakistan, aims to curb hostilities and secure negotiations. President Trump’s team—including Steve Witkoff, Marco Rubio, JD Vance, and Jared Kushner—is coordinating discussions. U.S. officials report progress, citing increased energy flow through the Strait of Hormuz. White House emphasizes diplomacy is ongoing while military operations continue. Iran’s UN mission declined comment. Regional actors, including Turkey, and the IAEA are also engaged, though U.S.-Iran talks remain indirect due to the lack of official diplomatic relations.

item.title: IRAN'S RESPONSE TO U.S. PEACE PROPOSAL EXPECTED FRIDAY, SOURCES SAY
item.description: IRAN'S RESPONSE TO U.S. PEACE PROPOSAL EXPECTED FRIDAY, SOURCES SAY

item.title:
EU AT RISK OF STAGFLATION FROM IRAN WAR
The Iran war threatens stagflation in the EU, says Economy Commissioner Valdis Dombrovskis. Short-term energy disruptions could cut growth by 0.4 points and raise inflation by 1 point, while prolonged disruption might reduce growth by 0.6 points this year and next. The EU’s November forecast projected 1.4% growth for 2026. Dombrovskis warned of “profound uncertainty” and stressed these figures are scenario-based, not predictions, but acknowledged the risk of a stagflationary shock to the bloc’s economy.
item.description:
EU AT RISK OF STAGFLATION FROM IRAN WAR

The Iran war threatens stagflation in the EU, says Economy Commissioner Valdis Dombrovskis. Short-term energy disruptions could cut growth by 0.4 points and raise inflation by 1 point, while prolonged disruption might reduce growth by 0.6 points this year and next. The EU’s November forecast projected 1.4% growth for 2026. Dombrovskis warned of “profound uncertainty” and stressed these figures are scenario-based, not predictions, but acknowledged the risk of a stagflationary shock to the bloc’s economy.

item.title:
SONY TO RAISE PLAYSTATION 5 PRICES IN APRIL
Sony will raise PlayStation 5 prices starting April 2, marking the second hike in under a year. In the U.S., the PS5 disc and digital editions increase by $100 to $649.99 and $599.99, respectively, while the PS5 Pro jumps $150 to $899.99. Sony cites global economic pressures and rising memory chip costs for the adjustment and says the step is needed to continue delivering high-quality gaming experiences. Price increases will also affect the U.K., Japan, and Europe.
item.description:
SONY TO RAISE PLAYSTATION 5 PRICES IN APRIL

Sony will raise PlayStation 5 prices starting April 2, marking the second hike in under a year. In the U.S., the PS5 disc and digital editions increase by $100 to $649.99 and $599.99, respectively, while the PS5 Pro jumps $150 to $899.99. Sony cites global economic pressures and rising memory chip costs for the adjustment and says the step is needed to continue delivering high-quality gaming experiences. Price increases will also affect the U.K., Japan, and Europe.

item.title: DOJ OFFICIAL SAYS THAT KASH PATEL MATERIAL PUBLISHED BY IRAN-LINKED HACKER GROUP APPEARS AUTHENTIC
item.description: DOJ OFFICIAL SAYS THAT KASH PATEL MATERIAL PUBLISHED BY IRAN-LINKED HACKER GROUP APPEARS AUTHENTIC

item.title:
🚨 IRAN-LINKED HACKERS BREACH FBI DIRECTOR'S EMAIL
Iran-linked group Handala Hack Team claims to have hacked FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal email, posting photos and his alleged resume online. A sample reviewed by Reuters shows emails from 2010–2019, mixing personal and work correspondence. A Justice Department official confirmed the breach, though details remain scarce. The FBI and the hackers have not commented further.
item.description:
🚨 IRAN-LINKED HACKERS BREACH FBI DIRECTOR'S EMAIL

Iran-linked group Handala Hack Team claims to have hacked FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal email, posting photos and his alleged resume online. A sample reviewed by Reuters shows emails from 2010–2019, mixing personal and work correspondence. A Justice Department official confirmed the breach, though details remain scarce. The FBI and the hackers have not commented further.

item.title: 🚨 IRAN-LINKED HACKERS CLAIM BREACH OF FBI DIRECTOR'S EMAIL: RTRS
item.description: 🚨 IRAN-LINKED HACKERS CLAIM BREACH OF FBI DIRECTOR'S EMAIL: RTRS

item.title:
OIL SHOCK COULD TRIGGER 10% STOCK SELLOFF
Guggenheim warns U.S. stocks could drop up to 10% if oil stays near $100 for several months.
The bigger risk isn’t inflation but a hit to consumer and investor sentiment—potentially breaking the retail “buy-the-dip” habit that has supported markets.
While oil may ease and stocks stabilize, elevated prices still tighten conditions and weigh on growth, leaving markets more fragile.
item.description:
OIL SHOCK COULD TRIGGER 10% STOCK SELLOFF

Guggenheim warns U.S. stocks could drop up to 10% if oil stays near $100 for several months.

The bigger risk isn’t inflation but a hit to consumer and investor sentiment—potentially breaking the retail “buy-the-dip” habit that has supported markets.

While oil may ease and stocks stabilize, elevated prices still tighten conditions and weigh on growth, leaving markets more fragile.

item.title:
CITI CUTS US STOCKS ON PROLONGED WAR RISK
Citi is reducing exposure to U.S. equities, warning the Iran conflict is unlikely to end quickly and is raising downside risks.
The bank cut its small-cap overweight to zero and moved overall equity allocation to neutral, citing oil shocks, weak macro signals, and tightening liquidity.
Markets are already under pressure, with major indexes nearing correction territory, while Citi warns limited panic so far leaves room for further declines if tensions escalate.
item.description:
CITI CUTS US STOCKS ON PROLONGED WAR RISK

Citi is reducing exposure to U.S. equities, warning the Iran conflict is unlikely to end quickly and is raising downside risks.

The bank cut its small-cap overweight to zero and moved overall equity allocation to neutral, citing oil shocks, weak macro signals, and tightening liquidity.

Markets are already under pressure, with major indexes nearing correction territory, while Citi warns limited panic so far leaves room for further declines if tensions escalate.

item.title:
MIDDLE EAST ESCALATION LIKELY, POLISH PM WARNS
Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk says a new escalation in the Middle East conflict could occur in the coming days.
Citing allied intelligence, he warned that stabilization is unlikely in the near term.
item.description:
MIDDLE EAST ESCALATION LIKELY, POLISH PM WARNS

Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk says a new escalation in the Middle East conflict could occur in the coming days.

Citing allied intelligence, he warned that stabilization is unlikely in the near term.

item.description: 🚨 NASDAQ 100 DROPS 10% FROM OCTOBER HIGH, SET TO ENTER CORRECTION

item.title:
ROUBINI: US MORE LIKELY TO ESCALATE IRAN WAR
Nouriel Roubini says there’s over a 50% chance the US escalates in Iran, as backing down risks greater economic and geopolitical damage.
He argues escalation could lead to regime collapse and a better medium-term outcome—despite short-term oil spikes—giving the US and Israel incentive to push forward.
Still, he warns prolonged conflict could hit global growth and trigger a 1970s-style oil shock, complicating central bank policy and potentially forcing new rate hikes.
item.description:
ROUBINI: US MORE LIKELY TO ESCALATE IRAN WAR

Nouriel Roubini says there’s over a 50% chance the US escalates in Iran, as backing down risks greater economic and geopolitical damage.

He argues escalation could lead to regime collapse and a better medium-term outcome—despite short-term oil spikes—giving the US and Israel incentive to push forward.

Still, he warns prolonged conflict could hit global growth and trigger a 1970s-style oil shock, complicating central bank policy and potentially forcing new rate hikes.

item.title: RUSSIAN OIL PRODUCERS WARN BUYERS ABOUT POSSIBLE FORCE MAJEURE ON OIL SUPPLIES AS BALTIC PORTS FACE DRONE ATTACKS, SOURCES SAY
item.description: RUSSIAN OIL PRODUCERS WARN BUYERS ABOUT POSSIBLE FORCE MAJEURE ON OIL SUPPLIES AS BALTIC PORTS FACE DRONE ATTACKS, SOURCES SAY

item.title:
$TSLA - TESLA–SPACEX MERGER EYED FOR 2027
Wedbush expects Tesla and SpaceX to merge by 2027 as ties between Elon Musk’s companies deepen.
Tesla already holds a stake in SpaceX, and a new joint chip facility further integrates their operations.
Despite likely FTC and DOJ hurdles, analysts say Musk’s goal is to unify more of the AI ecosystem—making a merger the “endgame.”
item.description:
$TSLA - TESLA–SPACEX MERGER EYED FOR 2027

Wedbush expects Tesla and SpaceX to merge by 2027 as ties between Elon Musk’s companies deepen.

Tesla already holds a stake in SpaceX, and a new joint chip facility further integrates their operations.

Despite likely FTC and DOJ hurdles, analysts say Musk’s goal is to unify more of the AI ecosystem—making a merger the “endgame.”

item.title:
U.S. INTELLIGENCE SHOWS ABOUT 1/3 OF IRAN'S MISSILES DESTROYED IN U.S. STRIKES WITH CERTAINTY, FIVE SOURCES TELL REUTERS
U.S. INTELLIGENCE SAYS ANOTHER THIRD OF IRAN'S MISSILES LIKELY DAMAGED, DESTROYED OR BURIED UNDERGROUND BY STRIKES SINCE FEB. 28 START OF WAR, FOUR SOURCES SAY
item.description:

item.title:
OIL SURGES AS HORMUZ DISRUPTION TIGHTENS SUPPLY
Oil prices are climbing as the Iran conflict looks set to extend into April, with attacks ongoing and traffic through the Strait of Hormuz largely stalled.
Around 8 million barrels per day remain offline, sharply tightening global supply, according to Saxo Bank’s Ole Hansen, with further pressure as tanker deliveries run out.
Brent nears $111 a barrel and WTI tops $96, putting March on track for a record monthly gain.
item.description:
OIL SURGES AS HORMUZ DISRUPTION TIGHTENS SUPPLY

Oil prices are climbing as the Iran conflict looks set to extend into April, with attacks ongoing and traffic through the Strait of Hormuz largely stalled.

Around 8 million barrels per day remain offline, sharply tightening global supply, according to Saxo Bank’s Ole Hansen, with further pressure as tanker deliveries run out.

Brent nears $111 a barrel and WTI tops $96, putting March on track for a record monthly gain.

item.title:
TRUMP “PUT” FADING AS MARKET UNCERTAINTY RISES
Barclays says markets are losing confidence in Trump’s support as constant policy reversals create “headline fatigue.”
While shifting deadlines around Iran briefly shook oil, bonds, and equities, investors still expect gains—possibly underpricing the risk of prolonged conflict.
The bank warns sustained tensions and higher oil prices could fuel stagflation, with slower growth and rising inflation into 2026.
item.description:
TRUMP “PUT” FADING AS MARKET UNCERTAINTY RISES

Barclays says markets are losing confidence in Trump’s support as constant policy reversals create “headline fatigue.”

While shifting deadlines around Iran briefly shook oil, bonds, and equities, investors still expect gains—possibly underpricing the risk of prolonged conflict.

The bank warns sustained tensions and higher oil prices could fuel stagflation, with slower growth and rising inflation into 2026.
